author: lolieatapple
description: Drag table column borders to manually resize column widths. Widths persist across sessions. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: lolieatapple
manifest.description: Drag table column borders to manually resize column widths. Widths persist across sessions.
manifest.name: Table Column Resize

name: Table Column Resize

releases.author: lolieatapple
releases.description:
## Fixes

- **Auto-fit column widths on first render.** Tables without saved widths no longer freeze to a one-time pixel measurement. CSS `table-layout: fixed; width: 100%` now distributes columns equally and stays responsive to the container, so the table no longer exceeds the page width.
- **PDF export with multiple tables no longer widens progressively.** The same root cause — locked pixel widths from initial measurement — was compounding across successive tables in the print container. Removing the freeze fixes the drift.
- **Scale saved widths to fit narrower containers.** When a table's saved widths total more than the available width (e.g., widths recorded in editor view, then exported to a narrower PDF page), columns are scaled down proportionally so the table fits without overflow.
- **Freeze columns on drag start.** With the auto-init removed, dragging one column would otherwise let CSS redistribute remaining space across the un-sized columns. All columns are now frozen at their current rendered widths the moment a drag begins.

releases.author: lolieatapple
releases.description:
## Fixes

- **Column resize now actually resizes the column.** Drag events were firing and column widths were being written, but Obsidian core / theme CSS was overriding `table-layout: fixed` (with `!important`), so the `<col>` widths were ignored. Fixed by moving the rule to the plugin's stylesheet with `!important`, and also applying widths to `<th>` as a fallback.
- **Drag survives re-renders.** In Live Preview, CM6 can re-render the table mid-drag, which was breaking the drag when listeners were bound to the handle element. Moved `pointermove` / `pointerup` to the document.

## Community plugin review fixes

- `void`-prefix unawaited `saveSettings()` calls.
- Remove unnecessary `HTMLTableElement` cast in the reading-view post-processor.
- Moved inline table/header styles (`table-layout`, `position`, `overflow`) to `styles.css`.

releases.author: lolieatapple
releases.description:
Initial release.

- Drag table column borders to resize widths
- Widths persist across sessions (Reading View, Live Preview, PDF export)
- Settings: configurable minimum column width, reset all saved widths
